prose: Troglitazone is correctly returned as a negative call under the adversarial_negative tier, and the reasoning rests on curated mechanism rather than raw activity. There is no curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and no registered neuro-AOP stressor status for this compound [E1], which is the sole basis for the call; the absence of an attached AOP pathway is consistent with that read. The 19 mechanistic assay hits, including 5 mitochondrial signals [E2], are precisely the kind of imposter bioactivity the engine deliberately does not act upon — corroboration at most, anti-diagnostic here, never decisive. Real-world signal is uninformative, with no assessable FAERS reports [E3]. Orthogonally, low predicted brain exposure (BBB-negative) [E4] offers an independent pharmacokinetic reason troglitazone is an implausible central driver. Taken together, the evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis supports non-flagging; no causal claim is made, and bioactivity alone does not overturn the curated verdict.

citations.detail: No curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and not a registered neuro-AOP stressor.
citations.reference: Davis AP, Wiegers TC, Johnson RJ, et al. Comparative Toxicogenomics Database (CTD): update 2023. Nucleic Acids Research. 2023;51(D1):D1257-D1262. Mechanistic scaffold: OECD Adverse Outcome Pathway Wiki. AOP-3 (WPHA/WNT Endorsed): inhibition of mitochondrial complex I of nigro-striatal neurons leads to parkinsonian motor deficits (Terron A, Bal-Price A, et al. Arch Toxicol. 2018;92:41-82). AOP-3 anchors a convergent mitochondrial-complex family — complex II/III/IV and redox cycling (AOPs 588/587/589/593, under development) — whose formal expansion EFSA funded in 2024 (call NP/EFSA/PREV/2024/02). For Alzheimer's: the endorsed AOP-12/48 (aging neurodegeneration + memory) anchor with a non-endorsed Tau/amyloid overlay (AOP-429/475).

citations.detail: Bioactive: 19 mechanistic assay hits (incl. 5 mitochondrial). This is the imposter signal the engine correctly does NOT act on.
citations.reference: U.S. EPA ToxCast/Tox21 invitroDB (v4.2), accessed through the NICEATM Integrated Chemical Environment (ICE). Active hitcalls only; queried on the salt-form-correct record.
U.S. FDA Adverse Event Reporting System (FAERS), via the openFDA drug/event API. Disproportionality (ROR) for the parkinsonism MedDRA cluster.

citations.detail: Low brain exposure (BBB-negative): an orthogonal pharmacokinetic reason it cannot be a driver.
citations.reference: Meng F, Xi Y, Huang J, Ayers PW. A curated diverse molecular database of blood-brain barrier permeability with chemical descriptors (B3DB). Scientific Data. 2021;8:289. Daina A, Zoete V. A BOILED-Egg to predict gastrointestinal absorption and brain access of small molecules. ChemMedChem. 2016;11(11):1117-1121.
